cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1075:func prepareArtifacts(projectRoot, wtPath, beadID, attemptID, baseRev string, opts ExecuteBeadOptions) (*executeBeadArtifacts, error) {

90	// package does not need to import a concrete store type in tests.
91	type BeadEventAppender interface {
92		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
93	}
94	
95	// ExecuteBeadOptions holds all parameters for an execute-bead worker run.
96	type ExecuteBeadOptions struct {
97		FromRev       string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
98		Harness       string
99		Model         string
100		Provider      string // explicit provider name; passed through to agent as -provider
101		ModelRef      string // catalog model-ref; passed through to agent as -model-ref
102		Effort        string
103		ContextBudget string // prompt budget: "", "minimal" (omits large governing docs for cheap-tier)
104		PromptFile    string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
105		WorkerID      string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
106		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, receives a kind:routing evidence entry after
107		// the agent run completes. This is the hook that feeds the cost-tiered
108		// routing analytics described in FEAT-routing-visibility.
109		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
110		// MirrorCfg, when non-nil and configured, enables mirroring the
111		// finalized .ddx/executions/<attempt>/ bundle to an out-of-band archive
112		// after the worker writes the result. Failures never affect the bead
113		// outcome — see executions_mirror.go.
114		MirrorCfg *config.ExecutionsMirrorConfig
115		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.DdxAgent used to dispatch the
116		// agent invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ExecuteBead
117		// constructs a fresh service from projectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
118		// Tests may inject a pre-built service to avoid loading provider config.
119		Service agentlib.DdxAgent
120		// AgentRunner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path for
121		// the agent invocation. Production callers leave this nil. Tests use this
122		// seam to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real service
123		// or provider chain. When set, it takes precedence over Service.
124		AgentRunner AgentRunner
125	}
126	
127	// ExecuteBeadRuntime is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBeadOptions.
128	// Durable knobs (Harness, Model, Provider, ModelRef, Effort,
129	// ContextBudget, MirrorCfg) are stripped — they live on
130	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via ExecuteBeadWithConfig's
131	// rcfg argument. Only non-serializable plumbing and per-invocation
132	// runtime intent remain.
133	//
134	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Runtime structs and §Stage 3.
135	type ExecuteBeadRuntime struct {
136		FromRev     string // base git revision (default: HEAD)
137		PromptFile  string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)
138		WorkerID    string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller
139		BeadEvents  BeadEventAppender
140		Service     agentlib.DdxAgent
141		AgentRunner AgentRunner
142	}
143	
144	// ExecuteBeadWithConfig is the SD-024 successor to ExecuteBead. It
145	// accepts a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
146	// ExecuteBeadRuntime (plumbing + per-invocation intent), assembles an
147	// equivalent ExecuteBeadOptions, and delegates to ExecuteBead.
148	// Behavior is identical to ExecuteBead with an equivalently-populated
149	// ExecuteBeadOptions.
150	//
151	// Stage 3 of SD-024: this function exists alongside ExecuteBead;
152	// production callers have not migrated yet.
153	func ExecuteBeadWithConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, beadID string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime ExecuteBeadRuntime, gitOps GitOps) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {
154		opts := ExecuteBeadOptions{
155			FromRev:       runtime.FromRev,
156			Harness:       rcfg.Harness(),
157			Model:         rcfg.Model(),
158			Provider:      rcfg.Provider(),
159			ModelRef:      rcfg.ModelRef(),
160			Effort:        rcfg.Effort(),
161			ContextBudget: rcfg.ContextBudget(),
162			PromptFile:    runtime.PromptFile,
163			WorkerID:      runtime.WorkerID,
164			BeadEvents:    runtime.BeadEvents,
165			MirrorCfg:     rcfg.MirrorConfig(),
166			Service:       runtime.Service,
167			AgentRunner:   runtime.AgentRunner,
168		}
169		return ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, opts, gitOps)
170	}
171	
172	// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.
173	// Merge is intentionally excluded — that belongs to the parent-side
174	// orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps). UpdateRef/DeleteRef are exposed here
175	// so landing-side helpers (e.g. BuildLandingGateContext) can pin a
176	// transient ref while running gate evaluation against an ephemeral
177	// worktree.
178	type GitOps interface {
179		HeadRev(dir string) (string, error)
180		ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)
181		WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error
182		WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error
183		WorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)
184		WorktreePrune(dir string) error
185		IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)
186		// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and
187		// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a
188		// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all
189		// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.
190		SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)
191		// UpdateRef updates ref in dir to sha. Used by landing helpers to pin a
192		// commit so a transient worktree can check it out without racing with
193		// other work that might prune it.
194		UpdateRef(dir, ref, sha string) error
195		// DeleteRef removes ref from dir. Used to unpin a transient ref after
196		// the consumer (e.g. an ephemeral worktree) is done with it.
197		DeleteRef(dir, ref string) error
198	}
199	
200	// AgentRunner runs an agent with the given options.
201	type AgentRunner interface {
202		Run(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)
203	}
204

207		DirAbs      string
208		DirRel      string
209		PromptAbs   string
210		PromptRel   string
211		ManifestAbs string
212		ManifestRel string
213		ResultAbs   string
214		ResultRel   string
215		ChecksAbs   string
216		ChecksRel   string
217		UsageAbs    string

219	}

222		AttemptID string                    `json:"attempt_id"`

227		Requested executeBeadRequested      `json:"requested"`

229		Governing []executeBeadGoverningRef `json:"governing,omitempty"`
230		Paths     executeBeadArtifactPaths  `json:"paths"`
231	}
232

234		Harness  string `json:"harness,omitempty"`

258	// GoverningRef is the exported alias for executeBeadGoverningRef, for use

260	type GoverningRef = executeBeadGoverningRef
261

270	}
271	
272	// Constants for worktree and artifact paths.
273	const (
274		ExecuteBeadWtDir       = ".ddx" // legacy; kept for compatibility
275		ExecuteBeadWtPrefix    = ".execute-bead-wt-"
276		ExecuteBeadArtifactDir = ".ddx/executions"
277	
278		// ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir is the subdirectory under $TMPDIR in which
279		// execute-bead creates its isolated worktrees. Keeping them outside
280		// the project tree prevents child processes (tests, hooks) running
281		// inside the worktree from mutating the parent repository's
282		// .git/config via inherited GIT_DIR.
283		ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir = "ddx-exec-wt"
284	)
285	
286	// executeBeadWorktreePath returns the absolute path where an execute-bead
287	// isolated worktree for (beadID, attemptID) should live.
288	func executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID string) string {
289		base := os.Getenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR")

16	func TestExecuteBead_RoutingEvidenceRecorded(t *testing.T) {
17		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
18		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
19		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
20	
21		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
22		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{"no-op"})
23	
24		beadStore := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
25		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
26		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
27	
28		cfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{
29			Harness: "script",
30			Model:   dirFile,
31		})
32		rcfg := cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
33		res, err := ExecuteBeadWithConfig(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, ExecuteBeadRuntime{
34			BeadEvents:  beadStore,
35			AgentRunner: runner,
36		}, gitOps)
37	
38		require.NoError(t, err)
39		require.NotNil(t, res)
40	
41		events, evErr := beadStore.Events(beadID)
42		require.NoError(t, evErr)
43	
44		var routingEvt *bead.BeadEvent
45		for i := range events {
46			if events[i].Kind == "routing" {
47				routingEvt = &events[i]
48				break
49			}
50		}
51		require.NotNil(t, routingEvt, "expected a kind=routing evidence event on bead %s", beadID)
52	
53		var body map[string]any
54		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(routingEvt.Body), &body),
55			"routing evidence body must be valid JSON")
56	
57		assert.Equal(t, "routing", routingEvt.Kind)
58		assert.NotEmpty(t, body["resolved_provider"], "resolved_provider must be set")
59		assert.Contains(t, body, "route_reason", "route_reason field must be present")
60		assert.NotEmpty(t, body["route_reason"], "route_reason must be non-empty")
61		assert.Contains(t, body, "fallback_chain", "fallback_chain field must be present")
62		assert.Contains(t, body, "resolved_model", "resolved_model field must be present")
63	
64		assert.Equal(t, "script", body["resolved_provider"])
65		assert.Equal(t, "direct-override", body["route_reason"])
66	}
67

93	func TestExecuteBead_RoutingEvidenceWithCommit(t *testing.T) {
94		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
95		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")
96		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
97	
98		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
99		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{
100			"append-line output.txt routing-evidence-test",
101			"commit chore: routing evidence test",
102		})
103	
104		beadStore := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
105		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
106		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
107		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
108	
109		cfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{
110			Harness: "script",
111			Model:   dirFile,
112		})
113		rcfg := cfg.Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
114		res, err := ExecuteBeadWithConfig(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, ExecuteBeadRuntime{
115			BeadEvents:  beadStore,
116			AgentRunner: runner,
117		}, gitOps)
118		require.NoError(t, err)
119		require.NotNil(t, res)
120	
121		_, _ = LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
122			LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
123				return Land(projectRoot, BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), RealLandingGitOps{})
124			},
125		})
126	
127		events, evErr := beadStore.Events(beadID)
128		require.NoError(t, evErr)
129	
130		found := false
131		for _, evt := range events {
132			if evt.Kind == "routing" {
133				found = true
134				var body map[string]any
135				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(evt.Body), &body))
136				assert.Equal(t, "script", body["resolved_provider"])
137				assert.Equal(t, "direct-override", body["route_reason"])
138				break
139			}
140		}
141		assert.True(t, found, "routing evidence must be recorded even on task_succeeded path")
142	}

91	// TestBeadConfigOpts names every durable knob the execute-bead worker
92	// reads via ResolvedConfig (per SD-024 §ExecuteBeadOptions →
93	// ExecuteBeadRuntime field classification). Tests must specify each
94	// field explicitly; there are no zero-value defaults that silently
95	// bypass real config.
96	//
97	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Test config constructors and §Stage 3.
98	type TestBeadConfigOpts struct {
99		Harness  string
100		Model    string
101		Provider string // CLI-only override, no durable home on AgentConfig
102		ModelRef string // CLI-only override, no durable home on AgentConfig
103		Effort   string // CLI-only override, no durable home on AgentConfig
104		Mirror   *ExecutionsMirrorConfig
105	}
106	
107	// NewTestConfigForBead returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with
108	// the matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose
109	// execute-bead-relevant accessors return the values supplied in opts.
110	// Pure CLI-override fields (Provider, ModelRef, Effort) have no
111	// durable home on AgentConfig and must be applied at Resolve time via
112	// CLIOverrides.
113	func NewTestConfigForBead(opts TestBeadConfigOpts) *Config {
114		cfg := &Config{
115			Version: "1.0",
116			Agent: &AgentConfig{
117				Harness: opts.Harness,
118				Model:   opts.Model,
119			},
120		}
121		if opts.Mirror != nil {
122			cfg.Executions = &ExecutionsConfig{Mirror: opts.Mirror}
123		}
124		return cfg
125	}
126	
127	// TestBeadOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
128	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForBead(opts), drive a Resolve call
129	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Provider,
130	// ModelRef, Effort) have no durable home on *Config; they are applied
131	// at Resolve time only.
132	func TestBeadOverrides(opts TestBeadConfigOpts) CLIOverrides {
133		return CLIOverrides{
134			Provider: opts.Provider,
135			ModelRef: opts.ModelRef,
136			Effort:   opts.Effort,
137		}
138	}
139	
140	// TestLoopOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
141	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForLoop(opts), drive a Resolve call
142	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Assignee,
143	// Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) have no durable home on *Config; they are
144	// applied at Resolve time only.

1	package agent
2	
3	// execute_bead_checkpoint_test.go — Tier-2 integration tests for FEAT-012 §22 +
4	// US-126 AC#1: when ExecuteBead starts and the parent worktree has uncommitted
5	// changes, DDx must capture them as a real commit on the current branch
6	// (the "checkpoint commit") and use the resulting HEAD as the effective base
7	// revision for the worker worktree. Caller's edits are preserved as a normal
8	// commit they can `git reset HEAD~` to recover.
9	//
10	// Clean parent worktrees must NOT spawn a redundant checkpoint commit.
11	
12	import (
13		"context"
14		"os"
15		"path/filepath"
16		"testing"
17	
18		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
19		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
20	)
21	
22	// TestExecuteBead_DirtyParentTree_CheckpointCommitted seeds a repo, makes the
23	// parent worktree dirty (one tracked-modified file, one untracked file),
24	// runs ExecuteBead, and asserts that:
25	//   - HEAD advanced by at least one commit before the worker worktree was
26	//     created (the checkpoint commit captures the caller's dirt)
27	//   - both the modified content and the untracked file are reachable in HEAD
28	//     (changes survived as a real commit, not discarded)
29	//   - the worker worktree's BaseRev points at that new HEAD (or a descendant
30	//     such as a tracker commit), not at the original seed commit
31	func TestExecuteBead_DirtyParentTree_CheckpointCommitted(t *testing.T) {
32		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
33		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
34	
35		// Make the parent dirty: modify the tracked seed file and add an
36		// untracked file. The checkpoint must capture both per IsDirty semantics.
37		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "seed.txt"),
38			[]byte("seed\nlocal modification\n"), 0o644))
39		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, "untracked.txt"),
40			[]byte("untracked content\n"), 0o644))
41	
42		headBefore := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
43		commitsBefore := gitCommitCount(t, projectRoot, "HEAD")
44	
45		// Directive does nothing observable — we only care about the pre-execution
46		// checkpoint behavior. An empty directive yields no_changes; that's fine.
47		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
48		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{})
49	
50		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
51		res, err := ExecuteBead(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
52			Harness:     "script",
53			Model:       dirFile,
54			AgentRunner: runner,
55		}, &RealGitOps{})
56		require.NoError(t, err)
57		require.NotNil(t, res)
58	
59		headAfter := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
60		commitsAfter := gitCommitCount(t, projectRoot, "HEAD")
61	
62		assert.NotEqual(t, headBefore, headAfter,
63			"HEAD must advance to capture the dirty caller worktree as a checkpoint commit")
64		assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, commitsAfter-commitsBefore, 1,
65			"at least one commit (the checkpoint) must land on the parent branch")
66	
67		// The modified seed and the untracked file must be reachable in HEAD —
68		// the checkpoint preserved the caller's work, did not discard it.
69		seedAtHead := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "show", "HEAD:seed.txt")
70		assert.Contains(t, seedAtHead, "local modification",
71			"tracked-modified content must be present in HEAD after checkpoint")
72		untrackedAtHead := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "show", "HEAD:untracked.txt")
73		assert.Contains(t, untrackedAtHead, "untracked content",
74			"untracked file must be tracked in HEAD after checkpoint (git add -A)")
75	
76		// BaseRev recorded on the result must be the new HEAD, not the original seed.
77		assert.NotEqual(t, headBefore, res.BaseRev,
78			"BaseRev must be the post-checkpoint HEAD, not the pre-checkpoint HEAD")
79	}
80	
81	// TestExecuteBead_CleanParentTree_NoSpuriousCheckpoint runs ExecuteBead against
82	// a clean parent worktree and asserts that no extra checkpoint commit is made
83	// beyond what other steps (CommitTracker) might add. The acceptance criterion
84	// is: clean parent trees do not create redundant checkpoint artifacts.
85	func TestExecuteBead_CleanParentTree_NoSpuriousCheckpoint(t *testing.T) {
86		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
87		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
88	
89		// Confirm we start clean.
90		status := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "status", "--porcelain")
91		require.Empty(t, status, "test setup invariant: parent must be clean")
92	
93		headBefore := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
94	
95		dirFile := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directive.txt")
96		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{})
97	
98		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
99		_, err := ExecuteBead(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
100			Harness:     "script",
101			Model:       dirFile,
102			AgentRunner: runner,
103		}, &RealGitOps{})
104		require.NoError(t, err)
105	
106		headAfter := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
107	
108		// CommitTracker may have added a tracker commit if .ddx/beads.jsonl
109		// changed during the attempt (e.g. claim metadata). What must NOT happen:
110		// a separate "checkpoint pre-execute-bead" commit when nothing was dirty.
111		if headBefore != headAfter {
112			// Inspect the diff: only beads.jsonl (or other tracker files) may
113			// differ. No checkpoint commit message in the log between these refs.
114			log := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "log", "--format=%s", headBefore+".."+headAfter)
115			assert.NotContains(t, log, "checkpoint pre-execute-bead",
116				"clean parent tree must not produce a checkpoint commit")
117		}
118	}

1	package agent
2	
3	// execute_bead_env_isolation_test.go — Regression test for "Bug A":
4	// when execute-bead is invoked with hook-contaminated GIT_DIR /
5	// GIT_WORK_TREE / GIT_INDEX_FILE in the environment, every git call
6	// inside the agent path must operate on its target dir (the project
7	// repo or its worktree), NOT on the inherited outer repo. Any callsite
8	// that reverts to bare exec.Command("git", ...) without scrubbing the
9	// env will mutate the outer bare repo and fail this test.
10	
11	import (
12		"context"
13		"crypto/sha256"
14		"encoding/hex"
15		"io/fs"
16		"os"
17		"path/filepath"
18		"sort"
19		"strings"
20		"testing"
21	
22		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
23	)
24	
25	// repoSnapshot records every file under root by relative path → sha256(content).
26	// Symlinks are recorded by their link target. Used to assert byte-for-byte
27	// equivalence of an outer bare repo before/after a contaminated execute-bead run.
28	type repoSnapshot struct {
29		files map[string]string // relPath -> sha256-hex of contents (or "L:"+target for symlinks)
30	}
31	
32	func snapshotDir(t *testing.T, root string) repoSnapshot {
33		t.Helper()
34		snap := repoSnapshot{files: map[string]string{}}
35		require.NoError(t, filepath.WalkDir(root, func(path string, d fs.DirEntry, err error) error {
36			if err != nil {
37				return err
38			}
39			if d.IsDir() {
40				return nil
41			}
42			rel, relErr := filepath.Rel(root, path)
43			if relErr != nil {
44				return relErr
45			}
46			if d.Type()&fs.ModeSymlink != 0 {
47				tgt, lerr := os.Readlink(path)
48				if lerr != nil {
49					return lerr
50				}
51				snap.files[rel] = "L:" + tgt
52				return nil
53			}
54			data, rerr := os.ReadFile(path)
55			if rerr != nil {
56				return rerr
57			}
58			sum := sha256.Sum256(data)
59			snap.files[rel] = hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
60			return nil
61		}))
62		return snap
63	}
64	
65	func (s repoSnapshot) diff(other repoSnapshot) []string {
66		var diffs []string
67		seen := map[string]bool{}
68		for k, v := range s.files {
69			seen[k] = true
70			ov, ok := other.files[k]
71			if !ok {
72				diffs = append(diffs, "removed: "+k)
73				continue
74			}
75			if ov != v {
76				diffs = append(diffs, "modified: "+k)
77			}
78		}
79		for k := range other.files {
80			if !seen[k] {
81				diffs = append(diffs, "added: "+k)
82			}
83		}
84		sort.Strings(diffs)
85		return diffs
86	}
87	
88	// noopRunner is a minimal AgentRunner that creates a single file in the
89	// worktree (so SynthesizeCommit fires) and exits 0.
90	type envIsoRunner struct{}
91	
92	func (envIsoRunner) Run(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error) {
93		if opts.WorkDir != "" {
94			path := filepath.Join(opts.WorkDir, "iso_marker.txt")
95			_ = os.WriteFile(path, []byte("iso\n"), 0o644)
96		}
97		return &Result{ExitCode: 0}, nil
98	}
99	
100	// TestExecuteBead_GitDirContaminatedEnv_LeavesOuterBareRepoUntouched is the
101	// regression test for Bug A: in-process execute-bead end-to-end (CommitTracker,
102	// WorktreeAdd, agent run, SynthesizeCommit, LandBeadResult preserve path) MUST
103	// NOT touch the outer bare repo named by inherited GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE /
104	// GIT_INDEX_FILE. Every git callsite in the agent path must scrub those vars
105	// (via internal/git.Command). If any callsite reverts to bare
106	// exec.Command("git", ...), this test will detect the mutation.
107	func TestExecuteBead_GitDirContaminatedEnv_LeavesOuterBareRepoUntouched(t *testing.T) {
108		// 1. Build the project working repo + bead store with scrubbed env (the
109		//    package-wide TestMain has already cleared GIT_*). newScriptHarnessRepo
110		//    seeds one bead and returns the project root.
111		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
112		const beadID = "ddx-int-0001"
113	
114		// 2. Build the fake outer bare repo that will play the role of the
115		//    hook-inherited GIT_DIR. Use a sibling tempdir so a buggy git invocation
116		//    that resolves the inherited GIT_DIR will land here, not in projectRoot.
117		outerBase := t.TempDir()
118		bareDir := filepath.Join(outerBase, "outer-bare.git")
119		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(bareDir, 0o755))
120		bareInit := runGitInteg(t, outerBase, "init", "--bare", "outer-bare.git")
121		_ = bareInit
122		fakeWork := filepath.Join(outerBase, "fake-work")
123		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(fakeWork, 0o755))
124		fakeIndex := filepath.Join(outerBase, "fake-index")
125		// Touch the index file so a buggy callsite that tries to read it can; we
126		// still expect the byte-identical assertion to catch any mutation.
127		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(fakeIndex, []byte{}, 0o644))
128	
129		// 3. Snapshot the bare repo BEFORE the contaminated execute-bead run.
130		before := snapshotDir(t, bareDir)
131		require.NotEmpty(t, before.files, "bare repo snapshot must include files (config, HEAD, etc.)")
132	
133		// 4. Contaminate the env: set GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / GIT_INDEX_FILE to
134		//    the outer bare repo. t.Setenv restores the prior (unset) state on
135		//    test exit. From this point on, any bare exec.Command("git", ...) in
136		//    production code will operate on bareDir instead of cmd.Dir.
137		t.Setenv("GIT_DIR", bareDir)
138		t.Setenv("GIT_WORK_TREE", fakeWork)
139		t.Setenv("GIT_INDEX_FILE", fakeIndex)
140	
141		// 5. Drive in-process execute-bead end-to-end. We do this directly (not via
142		//    scriptHarnessExecutor) so we can pass NoMerge: true to LandBeadResult,
143		//    exercising the preserve-ref path (UpdateRef in projectRoot) without
144		//    requiring a remote.
145		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
146		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
147	
148		res, err := ExecuteBead(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
149			Harness:     "script",
150			AgentRunner: envIsoRunner{},
151		}, gitOps)
152		require.NoError(t, err, "ExecuteBead should not fail under contaminated env")
153		require.NotNil(t, res)
154	
155		landing, lerr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
156			NoMerge: true,
157		})
158		require.NoError(t, lerr, "LandBeadResult should not fail under contaminated env")
159		require.NotNil(t, landing)
160	
161		// Sanity: the run should have either preserved (NoMerge with commits) or
162		// reported no-changes. Either way, the bare repo must be untouched.
163		switch landing.Outcome {
164		case "preserved", "no-changes":
165			// ok
166		default:
167			t.Fatalf("unexpected landing outcome %q (reason=%q); test cannot prove env isolation", landing.Outcome, landing.Reason)
168		}
169	
170		// 6. Snapshot the bare repo AFTER and assert byte-for-byte equivalence.
171		after := snapshotDir(t, bareDir)
172		if diffs := before.diff(after); len(diffs) > 0 {
173			t.Fatalf("outer bare repo was mutated by execute-bead under contaminated env "+
174				"(this means a git callsite in the agent path inherited GIT_DIR=%s and "+
175				"operated on the outer repo instead of cmd.Dir):\n  %s",
176				bareDir, strings.Join(diffs, "\n  "))
177		}
178	
179		// 7. Defence-in-depth: the bare repo's config bytes specifically must be
180		//    byte-identical (the most common Bug A symptom is core.bare flipping or
181		//    stray core.worktree entries appearing).
182		cfgPath := filepath.Join(bareDir, "config")
183		cfgBefore := before.files["config"]
184		cfgAfter := after.files["config"]
185		if cfgBefore == "" {
186			t.Fatalf("bare repo config not captured in snapshot (path=%s)", cfgPath)
187		}
188		if cfgBefore != cfgAfter {
189			raw, _ := os.ReadFile(cfgPath)
190			t.Fatalf("bare repo config bytes mutated\n  path: %s\n  contents now:\n%s", cfgPath, string(raw))
191		}
192	}
